title: cephalanthera damasonium subsp. caucasica
intro: Cephalanthera damasonium subsp. caucasica, also known as the Caucasian Helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Caucasus region and parts of Eastern Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cephalanthera damasonium subsp. caucasica include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate congestion, and soothe respiratory tract irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions or decoctions, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
